definition: Put (someone) to death by nailing or binding them to a cross
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns, you shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.
speech: Cross of Gold Speech (Democratic National Convention)

definition: Human beings considered collectively; the human race
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns, you shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.
speech: Cross of Gold Speech (Democratic National Convention)

definition: The state of being prosperous
example: There are two ideas of government. There are those who believe that, if you will only legislate to make the well-to-do prosperous, their prosperity will leak through on those below.

definition: Make or enact laws
example: There are two ideas of government. There are those who believe that, if you will only legislate to make the well-to-do prosperous, their prosperity will leak through on those below.

definition: Turn up the earth of (an area of land) with a plow, especially before sowing
example: If there is no struggle, there is no progress. Those who profess to favor freedom, and yet depreciate agitation, are men who want crops without plowing up the ground.

definition: A formal written request, typically one signed by many people, appealing to authority with respect to a particular cause
example: We have petitioned, and our petitions have been scorned; we have entreated, and our entreaties have been disregarded.

definition: A feeling or expression of contempt
example: We have petitioned, and our petitions have been scorned; we have entreated, and our entreaties have been disregarded.

definition: An earnest or humble request
example: In his address, the orator urged his listeners to show entreaty when facing the greatest challenges.

definition: A system allowing the unrestricted currency of two metals (e.g., gold and silver) as legal tender at a fixed ratio to each other
example: If they say bimetallism is good, but that we cannot have it until other nations help us, we reply that, instead of having a gold standard because England has, we will restore bimetallism.

definition: A level of quality or attainment
example: If they say bimetallism is good, but that we cannot have it until other nations help us, we reply that, instead of having a gold standard because England has, we will restore bimetallism.

definition: Open resistance; bold disobedience
example: In his address, the orator urged his listeners to show defiance when facing the greatest challenges.

definition: A stiff, sharp-pointed woody projection on the stem or other part of a plant
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns, you shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.
speech: Cross of Gold Speech (Democratic National Convention)

definition: A circular ornamental headdress worn by a monarch as a symbol of authority
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns, you shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.
speech: Cross of Gold Speech (Democratic National Convention)

definition: Work, especially hard physical work
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns, you shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.
speech: Cross of Gold Speech (Democratic National Convention)

definition: A person's forehead
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns, you shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.
speech: Cross of Gold Speech (Democratic National Convention)

definition: The action of conquering a place or people
example: We do not come as aggressors. Our war is not a war of conquest; we are fighting in the defense of our homes, our families, and posterity.

definition: All future generations of people
example: We do not come as aggressors. Our war is not a war of conquest; we are fighting in the defense of our homes, our families, and posterity.

definition: A person who urges others to protest or rebel
example: In his address, the orator urged his listeners to show agitator when facing the greatest challenges.

definition: A yellow precious metal, the chemical element of atomic number 79
example: You shall not press down upon the brow of labor this crown of thorns, you shall not crucify mankind upon a cross of gold.
speech: Cross of Gold Speech (Democratic National Convention)

definition: Bringing or worthy of honor
example: In giving freedom to the slave, we assure freedom to the free — honorable alike in what we give, and what we preserve.
